description_long: This factory building is a large, L-shaped structure with a combination of brick and concrete facades. The main section features expansive rows of industrial windows, suggesting ample natural light for interior workspaces. Two prominent vertical brick towers anchor the ends of the longer wing, while the shorter wing has a more segmented appearance with smaller window groupings. The roof is flat and appears to have some minor rooftop installations, though no major machinery is visible. The building's exterior is characterized by utilitarian design, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. The overall appearance is consistent with mid-20th-century industrial architecture, suitable for manufacturing or warehousing in an urban environment.
description: A large, L-shaped brick and concrete factory building with extensive industrial windows and flat roofs, designed for manufacturing or warehousing. The structure features prominent end towers and a utilitarian, functional style.
description_view_0: This view highlights the L-shaped layout, showing both the long main wing and the shorter perpendicular section, with visible rooftop details and segmented window groupings.
description_view_1: The main facade is visible here, dominated by wide rows of large industrial windows and flanked by two tall brick towers at each end.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the depth of the building, showing the rear of the main wing and the side of the shorter wing, with less window detail visible.
description_view_3: The rear and side of the building are shown, with the shorter wing's segmented structure and smaller windows more prominent, and the main wing's industrial windows still visible.
